Chat Edit: 'Please make sure the voice streaming to text works anywhere that text can be used/issued and the AI can speak in volume 
'

Chat Edit: 'No minimal conversions / imlementations / placeholders / simulations / mock code , systematically remove ALL QT and replace it with our own custom from scratch code regardless of if its MASM or C++, begin, we have 1,000,001 turns to complete the entire IDE audit and conversions'

Chat Edit: 'Yes, a fully working CLI accessible GUI would be nice to have along with the actual GUI and its features and they should mirror each other entirely in functionality, no simp or skipping or minimalization make the cli next to the terminal pwsh'

Chat Edit: 'Suggested next steps

Wire UI callbacks to the core logic – connect the native widgets (buttons, combo boxes, text input, chat view) to AgentOrchestra methods (sendMessage, startVoiceInput, stopVoiceInput, setVoiceAccent, etc.).
Implement chat view updates – flesh out Native_AppendChatMessage (timestamp, user/agent styling) and ensure scrolling works.
Add structured logging / telemetry – introduce a lightweight logger (file‑based, with log levels) and expose counters for messages processed, voice events, errors.
Create a small integration test – launch the GUI, simulate a few user actions, and verify that the chat view updates and the orchestration callbacks fire.
Update documentation – build instructions for the MinGW/CMake workflow, and usage notes for the CLI vs. GUI.'

Chat Edit: 'Suggested next steps

Wire UI callbacks to the core logic – connect the native widgets (buttons, combo boxes, text input, chat view) to AgentOrchestra methods (sendMessage, startVoiceInput, stopVoiceInput, setVoiceAccent, etc.).
Implement chat view updates – flesh out Native_AppendChatMessage (timestamp, user/agent styling) and ensure scrolling works.
Add structured logging / telemetry – introduce a lightweight logger (file‑based, with log levels) and expose counters for messages processed, voice events, errors.
Create a small integration test – launch the GUI, simulate a few user actions, and verify that the chat view updates and the orchestration callbacks fire.
Update documentation – build instructions for the MinGW/CMake workflow, and usage notes for the CLI vs. GUI.'

Chat Edit: 'Next phases (targeted)

Wire menu/toolbar actions fully via QMenuBar::Dispatch and ensure WM_COMMAND covers buttons/combos consistently.
Implement NativeTabWidget and integrate in PaintTabbedEditor and ChatTabbedInterface for visual tabs.
Build paint UI: native canvas and tool palette (colors, brush/eraser), hooking to PaintCanvas.
Dialogs/settings: add QMessageBox::question using MessageBox, and persist window state with QSettings-like INI/Registry.
Telemetry: extend Logger with counters and latencies; optional Prometheus/OpenTelemetry hooks per AI Toolkit instructions.
Would you like me to proceed with the native tab widget implementation next, or finish wiring all menu/toolbar actions and event dispatch first?


Start with the last one and work forward'
